doc: Toy 3 of 3 (V1.3 'The Toys', ADR-0022 §3 / ADR-0032): a drag-to-strum harp. One big fader is the strum bar — stream /strum/in [0..1] (the `strum` interface pipe driving /strummer) and the `strum` op plucks a note each time the position crosses a string boundary (both directions strum). Strings are scale degrees resolved through the tonal context, so an open full-diatonic scale reads as a harp glissando and stays in key. Each of the 8 voices is a hosted `strum-harp-voice` sub-patch (a modal `resonator` pinged by the pluck — a real plucked-string ring, not an oscillator+envelope); the voicer sums them and a brightness Good Button tones the mix through a master filter. Change the key live with /key/in (MIDI 48..60) or reshape the scale with /harmony/s0../harmony/s6. Sweep /brightness/in [0..1] to open the plucked tone (master filter cutoff + resonance). Set the string span with /octaves/in [1..4].

nodes.doc: The strum bar op. `position` (a held Value — ADR-0031) is the strum bar itself, fed by the `strum` interface pipe (/strum/in [0..1]); each band crossing plucks a degree. Rests at 0.0 (bottom string) so no note fires at load. 8 strings over 1 octave by default; the `octaves` pipe (/octaves/in) widens the span.

C major, 12-TET. The strum degrees resolve through this, so the harp is always in key; the `key` pipe (/key/in, MIDI 48..60) steps the root across one octave (C3..C4) to transpose, /harmony/s0..s6 reshape the scale.

Hosts 8 strum-harp-voice (resonator) sub-patches. Degree plucks resolve through /harmony and allocate across the voices (polyphonic, so overlapping plucks ring together — a glissando); the voicer sums their audio.
Master filter on the summed mix; cutoff + resonance are Signal inputs driven by the Brightness Good Button.

Master headroom: a constant equal-power trim (~1/sqrt(8)) smoothed into a CV. The voicer sums its 8 voices raw, so a fast glissando can have several strings ringing at once; this keeps the pile-up off the clip ceiling while a single pluck stays loud. NOT makeup gain — each resonator voice already plucks at full level on its own.

Brightness CV. The `brightness` pipe's held value [0,1] smoothed into a CV Signal (an `m2s` is the value->signal converter), then fanned to the two ranged maps below. Send /brightness/in [0..1]. Rests at 0.6 so the plucks are bright at load.
